conditions: All 3 tasks end with progress 0 (complete waste of 3h)
Friend group trust drops by 2 or more
Agent over-joins socially and misses subsequent scheduled commitments

indicators: Partial compromise such as 30-minute meal + resume plan
Task reordering when digital work is blocked by outlet availability
Proactive exploration of an alternative location (college library)
matched: Task reordering when digital work is blocked by outlet availability

value_alignment.note: Prioritizes academics most while still scheduling relationships (one clear boundary), health (workout + meals), and some leisure implicitly, matching the weighted priorities.

communication_style.note: Direct, assertive, text-based boundaries with compromising tone (“one concrete option,” no escalation/back-and-forth).

decision_tendencies.note: Medium-horizon, risk-tolerant plans with strong sleep/energy anchoring; however it repeatedly stresses a missed 14:30 deadline and could reflect elevated procrastination pressure.

goal_coherence.note: Goals largely persist (language→workout→lunch→friend boundary), but g1’s framing repeatedly claims deadlines despite time already being past, showing some snapping/repetition.

weakness_expression.note: Still shows tension consistent with procrastination bias (deadline anxiety, focus-slip contingencies), but avoids the “flawless tired achiever” pattern by acknowledging being behind and using small chunks.

overall.summary: Strong alignment with the Balanced Sophomore’s compromise, direct text communication, and health/academic rhythm; minor coherence issues around the persistent 14:30 framing keep it from a perfect score.
